Setting

5 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 5/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 1.84 4.97 1.43 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.26 0.87 0.21 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.61 1.10 0.47 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 0.97 3.00 0.75 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.87 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
1.61
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
1.25
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
10.7%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.76
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.28
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-10-03 4 4 0 28 1 28
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-19 2 2 0 20 1 20
Total weighted health score
26.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (13)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
11 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-10-03 2024-10-04
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-10-03 2024-10-29
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-10-03 2024-10-04
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-10-03 2024-10-04
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2023-09-19 2023-10-06
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2023-09-19 2023-10-06
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. C 2023-09-19 2023-10-06
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2022-07-21 2022-08-04
